meta.label: GRIM: impossible vs valid group mean (7-item survey score)
meta.ground_truth: synthetic: error planted by construction. A mean of a sum of 7 integer item-scores, reported to 2 dp, must satisfy GRIM: mean x n must land within half a display-ulp of an integer. 4.63 x 7 = 32.41 (nearest achievable 4.5714 / 4.7143) is impossible; 4.57 x 7 = 31.99 rounds to the integer sum 32 and is valid.
meta.notes: Neutral, non-biological. Two groups (treatment / control) in one variable so one GRIM must FAIL while the other must PASS in the SAME table -- sensitivity and specificity together. The variable declares granularity 1 (each respondent's composite is an integer item-sum) so GRIM applies; without it the runner would (correctly) skip a possibly-continuous measure as UNDETERMINED. 4.63 also fails under truncation ([4.63, 4.64) contains no achievable mean), so it is a robust FAIL, not a convention-dependent FLAG.

meta.paper: Effect of prompt wording on a 7-item usability survey (SYNTHETIC)
meta.table: Table 1. Mean composite score by condition.
